column2: Set up the firebase settings in environment.yml
column5: The settings are set up successfully
column6:
Open environment.yml
Set settings from firebase

name: string
platform:
config:
  PushMessages__UseFirebaseCloudMessaging: true
  PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__Type: string
  PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__ProjectId: string
  PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__PrivateKeyId: string
  PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__ClientEmail: string
  PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__ClientId: string
  PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__AuthUri: string
  PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__TokenUri: string
  PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__AuthProviderX509CertUrl: string
  PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__ClientX509CertUrl: string
  PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__UniverseDomain: string
  PushMessages__FcmReceiverOptions__ApiKey: string
  PushMessages__FcmReceiverOptions__AuthDomain: string
  PushMessages__FcmReceiverOptions__ProjectId: string
  PushMessages__FcmReceiverOptions__StorageBucket: string
  PushMessages__FcmReceiverOptions__MessagingSenderId: string
  PushMessages__FcmReceiverOptions__AppId: string
  PushMessages__FcmReceiverOptions__VapidKey: string
secretConfig:
  PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__PrivateKey: firebase-private-key-new
column12: Browser notifications
column13:
1. Open https://console.firebase.google.com/
Click Add project
Enter project name
Click Create project
When the project is created, click Continue

2. Sender settings
Click Project settings
Click Service accounts
Click Generate new private key
In the popup window click Generate key
Open the downloaded JSON file and copy all fields to the corresponding fields in the
appSettings.json
Copy private key value and put the resulting value in the Key Vault (Azure portal)

3. Receiver settings
On the Project Overview page click Add Web app
Enter application name
Click Register app
In the Add Firebase SDK section you will find settings that should be placed in the
PushMessages.FcmReceiverOptions section in the appSettings.json
Click Continue to console
Click Project settings
Click Cloud Messaging
In the Web Push certificates section click Generate key pair

3. Save settings in environment.yml and push the pull request
4. Open the Frontend application > Console

- User can recover from all error states

column2: BUG-001: Pickup Map Width After Search (VCST-4518)
column6:
• PR #2138 deployed to test environment
  • Test URL: https://vcst-qa-storefront.govirto.com
  • Cart contains at least one product
  • Multiple pickup locations configured
column12: BOPIS (Pickup locations) > Pickup locations. Search and Facet Filtering > VCST-4499 BOPIS Map Modal
column14:
Step Description:
  1. Add product to cart and navigate to cart page
  2. Select ""Pickup"" under SHIPPING DETAILS
  3. Click ""Select pickup address"" button
  4. Verify ""Pick points"" modal opens with list (left) and map (right)
  5. Measure initial map width - verify approximately 50% of modal width
  6. Enter search term ""Westent foxpost"" in search field
  7. Click Search button
  8. Verify ""Pickup points not found"" message displays
  9. CRITICAL CHECK: Verify map maintains at least 40-50% of modal width (not ~30% or less)
  10. Verify map remains usable and visible
  11. Click ""Reset search"" button
  12. Verify map returns to normal width (~50%)
  13. Enter search term ""South Schuylershire"" in search field
  14. Click Search button
  15. Verify one location result displays
  16. CRITICAL CHECK: Verify map maintains at least 40-50% of modal width with valid results
  17. Click on ""South Schuylershire"" location in list
  18. Verify ""Pick point info"" card opens
  19. CRITICAL CHECK: Verify info card has sufficient space and readable layout
  20. Verify map width remains stable throughout all operations
Expected Result:

EXPECTED (AFTER FIX):

  • Initial state: Map occupies ~50% of modal width
  • After empty search: Map maintains at least 40-50% width
  • After valid search: Map maintains at least 40-50% width
  • After reset: Map returns to ~50% width
  • Info card: Opens with sufficient space, readable content
  • Map never becomes unusably narrow (<40% width)
  • List/empty state capped at reasonable width (max 350-400px)
